channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced PS5 Pro Features Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Ubisoft has revealed all the PS5 and PS5 Pro features players can take advantage of with Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced.

The highly anticipated remake of one of the most popular titles in the Assassin’s Creed series is set to release in less than two weeks. Players can look forward to enhanced visuals and improved gameplay. Additionally, the PS5 Pro will offer further enhancements to this experience.

Black Flag Resynced PS5 and PS5 Pro Graphics Modes

Starting with the PS5 version, “Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced” has been remade using the latest Anvil engine. This upgrade brings several enhancements, including options for 60 FPS, HDR support, Dolby Atmos, DualSense haptic feedback, and more. The PS5 will offer three graphics modes, all featuring a 2160p upscaled resolution:

  • Performance Mode: 60 FPS with standard Ray Tracing
  • Fidelity Mode: 30 FPS with extended Ray Tracing
  • Balanced Mode: 40 FPS with extended Ray Tracing

Ray Tracing Global Illumination will be enabled for all graphics modes on the PS5 and PS5 Pro. Ray-Traced Specular Reflections are available in Balanced and Fidelity modes on PS5 and across all graphics modes on PS5 Pro. PS5 Pro will use significantly more GPU power, providing enhanced ray-tracing performance and having more “headroom for higher fidelity and more stable performance.”

  • More Insider Gaming: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced on Nintendo Switch 2 Console Platform Status

PS5 Pro Will Feature Strand-Based Hair Simulation for Black Flag Resynced

Moreover, the PS5 Pro version of Black Flag Resynced will simulate and render individual hair strands rather than using traditional hair cards. This stand-based hair will be available for Edward in all modes, for the crowd in Fidelity mode, and cinematics will use this system for all characters regardless of the mode.

Lastly, the upgraded PSSR will provide better image quality and improved reconstruction, reducing the visual gap between performance and fidelity modes. Check out the PS5 Pro modes for Black Flag Resynced below; all will provide 2160p upscaled resolution with enhanced PSSR.

  • Performance Mode: 60 FPS with extended Ray Tracing
  • Fidelity Mode: 30 FPS with extended Ray Tracing
  • Balanced Mode: 40 FPS with extended Ray Tracing

For those wondering, Standard ray tracing will compute global illumination for diffuse lighting throughout the game world. Extended will bring full support of ray tracing to compute global illumination for diffuse lighting and reflective surfaces throughout the game world. All details were provided through a PlayStation blog post.

What are your thoughts on Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced PS5 and PS5 Pro features? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Battlefield 6 XP Token timer receiving a much-needed change. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Every Player at the Esports Nations Cup Valorant Tournament
item.content:encoded:
See which Valorant pros are set to play at the inaugural Esports Nations Cup 2026 Valorant tournament!

Joining the 16 invited teams, 15 teams have qualified through regional qualifiers, with players such as Masters London MVP Bruno ‘Neon’ Rodríguez, Gentle Mates’ Martin ‘marteen’ Pátek, and Sentinels legend Tyson ‘TenZ’ Ngo qualifying with their respective nations.

The list of players includes VCT stars, rising Challengers talent, and six women including G2 Gozen’s Michaela ‘mimi’ Lintrup, so check out the list below to see if your favorite players will be there.

Full list of players for Valorant at the Esports Nations Cup

  • UNITED STATES OF AMERICA – trent, valyn, leaf, zekken, skuba, Ethan, and Cryocells
  • REPUBLIC OF KOREA – iZu, BuZz, Francis, Munchkin, Ivy, and Dambi 
  • PEOPLE’S REPUBLIC OF CHINA – ZmjKK, CHICHOO, nobody, Knight, slowly, K1Ra, and Lysoar
  • CANADA – TenZ, mada, eeiu, Marved, JonahP, koalanoob, and Zander
  • TÜRKIYE – Alfajer, Ruxic, Lar0k, RieNs, xeus, Wo0t, and s0pp
  • BRAZIL – aspas, artzin, spikeziN, Less, Sacy, Sato, and lukxo
  • SINGAPORE – Deryeon, Jinggg, nephh, STYRON, wayne, Vera, and JayH
  • POLAND – kayak, kamo, starxo, PROFEK, westside, paTiTek, and UNFAKE
  • PHILIPPINES – Zeus, kellyS, xavi8k, invy, PatMen, Markyyy, and Wild0reoo
  • GREAT BRITAIN – Lime, Severe, benjyfishy, keiko, Ticey, and YaBoiLewis
  • CHINESE TAIPEI – Nicc, SpiritZ1, WsLeo, Spring, S1Mon, and Yuicaw
  • MALAYSIA – d4v41, theDoctorr, xan, Riza, Fixy, toggle, and aplycs
  • FINLAND – Jamppi, Derke, iluri, Famsii, H1ber, hoody, and Snowi
  • LITHUANIA – Boo, MiniBoo, Koshmaras, Kajuks, Destrian, roxie, and vakk
  • THAILAND – killua, Primmie, JitboyS, PTC, Apinya, and Kadoom
  • CHILE – kiNgg, erde, Mazino, keznit, Tunaso, Shyy, and david
  • JAPAN – Meiy, SugarZ3ro, Caedye, Aace, Dep, yatsuka, and Yuran
  • INDIA – venka, Hoax, Lightningfast, Hellff, Deecee, karam1l, and Techno
  • HUNGARY – szimpli, Chadi, shinnok, bravo, kyoukei, beNca, and n0rth
  • CZECHIA – Minny, marteen, MAGNUM, MONSTEERR, alexiiik, Jesse, and Nun
  • FRANCE – K4davra, sh1n, TakaS, CyvOph, Veqaj, mikee, and TITOUNE
  • DENMARK – AvovA, Pa1ze, KPZET, Noia, mimi, Bjorn, and Rawn3n
  • SAUDI ARABIA – ExiT, Zimo, KHaaLiD, AL7B, Fighter, ada, and madv
  • SOUTH AFRICA – THEWY, TRAVIS, ASTRAL, SORA, RUDY, FRESH, and MORGS
  • COLOMBIA – Keii, Gato, Devost, Zeypher, Ichiwin, Zelt, and Yasikof
  • DOMINICAN REPUBLIC – Mininiwde, Xhowi, Virtyy, Dobsha, Megumi, Buffala, and Janel
  • ARGENTINA – Saadhak, Neon, dante, rubkkoide, ruso, darky, and jotinha
  • PERU – Xuto, Sayled, fab, Jeffrey, Grimmjow, Lied, and daddy
  • VIETNAM – bucute, f1cio, YESicaN, n1zzy, Pr0digy, Crus, and Hyuse
  • NEW ZEALAND – Blessed, FETA, BiggieCheese, RESLYDE, Swifty, tisoyy, and Iskander
  • UNITED ARAB EMIRATES – Ttyke, Gon, Aim Hunter, Fr1, Akai, Ebeee, and HeavyRain
  • Fancy yourself an esports expert? Start betting with a $20 deposit at Polymarket and receive a $50 bonus!

How each team qualified for the Esports Nations Cup

In addition to the 16 invited nations, 15 teams in total qualified through regional qualifiers. They qualified through the following pathways:

  • Asia: Japan & India
  • Eastern Europe: Hungary & Czechia
  • Western Europe: France & Denmark
  • Middle East/North Africa: Saudi Arabia
  • Sub-Saharan Africa: South Africa
  • North America: Colombia & Dominican Republic
  • South America: Argentina & Peru
  • Southeast Asia/Oceania: Vietnam & New Zealand
  • Host Region Slot: United Arab Emirates

That means that 31 of the 32 teams for ENC 2026 Valorant are decided, with one spot reserved for a wildcard ‘Solidarity Slot.’ Solidarity slots are special wildcards given to underrepresented regions in their title, with the beneficiary still to be decided.

Let us know what you think of the move on the Insider Gaming Discord


For other news, see the EWC 2026 Valorant groups and sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

item.title: Battlefield 6 XP Token Timer Gets Long-Awaited Change
item.content:encoded:
UPDATE: A new post by the official BattlefieldComms account on X (formerly Twitter) reveals that the in-game message about XP Boosters was sent as an “error”. The message has been removed, and there are “no changes to how XP Boosters currently work.”

ORIGINAL: A big change has been made to Battlefield 6 XP tokens, as they will no longer count down in real time and will follow in-game time.

Battlefield 6 is set to receive a significant update with version 1.3.3.0, scheduled for release on June 30. This update will introduce major adjustments to gunplay, including changes to recoil, time-to-kill (TTK), limb damage, and more. Along with that, players are receiving a long-awaited update to XP Boost, which players are sure to appreciate.

Battlefield 6 XP Tokens Will No Longer Count Down in Real Time

Spotted by CharlieIntel on X (formerly Twitter), Battlefield 6 is implementing a change to its XP tokens. These boosters will now be based on in-game time rather than real time. This means the countdown for boosters will be tracked as a percentage of the match length while the booster is active. The final Match Bonus XP will be multiplied by this percentage.

If a player’s XP booster expires during a match, they will still receive a multiplier for their Match Bonus XP. This multiplier will be calculated based on how long the booster was active during the match. Additionally, the Booster menu has been relocated to the Lobby for easier access and tracking.

  • More Insider Gaming: EA Has Shut Down Servers For An Underrated Battlefield Game

Players will surely appreciate the change to XP booster tokens in Battlefield 6, as this has been a common complaint in many games, including Call of Duty. Previously, when boosters were real-time-based, players felt pressured to play as much as possible before the token expired. With the new change, players can enjoy the game at their own pace without worrying about wasting their XP tokens.

What are your thoughts on the Battlefield 6 XP tokens timer change? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about GTA 6 marketing links only showing PS5 pre-orders. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: GTA 6 Marketing Links Only Showing PS5 Pre-Orders on Rockstar Games Website, Not XBOX
item.content:encoded:
Oh boy, the GTA 6 and Sony PlayStation narrative has taken an interesting turn. It’s been no secret since pre-orders went live that PlayStation has been heavily promoting Rockstar’s new game, and from different viewpoints, it would look like the feeling is mutual.

Sony has claimed GTA 6 plays ‘Best on PS5.’ Additionally, we’ve learned that GTA 6 will be PS5 Pro Enhanced, that there will be GTA 6 PlayStation-Exclusive features, and also that GTA 6 will have ‘Improved Performance’ and ‘More Stable FPS’ on PS5 Pro.

Following all of this up are reports that the GTA 6 website is only favoring PlayStation branding, but there’s more to it than that.

GTA 6 Default Website Shows Both PlayStation & Xbox

Social media is a hotbed for all GTA 6 news, theories, and misinformation right now.

New claims and screenshots surfaced showing the main website featuring the ‘Plays Best on PS’ marketing, and also the eye-opening omission of XBOX pre-orders—only advertising PS5 pre-orders instead.

  • More Insider Gaming: GTA 6 Has Taken Over The PS5 Home Screen

To be clear, these are special marketing links that DO only highlight Sony and PlayStation—Shown here by @videotech.

But this is not the case for everyone, and if you search for the main Rockstar Games website on your own, you’ll see the differences.

Check out both examples below:

  • Here’s a PS5 marketing link for GTA 6.
  • Here’s the normal Rockstar Games website without any marketing.

The first link is PlayStation-themed; the second is what you’ll see normally.

It’s only very specific links that show the PS5 branding and advertising, but make no mistake, it does exist, and Sony does seem to be getting the sweeter end of the deal with GTA 6.

  • More Insider Gaming: GTA 6 Has Practically No Loading Screens on PS5

Presumably for a price.

While we wait for Trailer 3, here’s GTA Trailer 2!

How much do you think Sony’s arrangement in place with Rockstar and GTA 6 is costing? Let us know via the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, read about XBOX gamers using Microsoft Rewards to get GTA 6 for free, and be sure to sign up for our Newsletter.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Guns of Eschaton Reveal Uncovers Souls-Like Apocalyptic FPS Set in the ‘Dying West’
item.content:encoded:
In an all-new unveiling of an impressive and landmark Souls-like FPS, 4Divinity and Eschatology Entertainment have lifted the lid on Guns of Eschaton. It’s an innovative, intriguing shooter set in the apocalyptic world concocted by the late Viktor Antonov, who once worked on the likes of Half-Life 2 and Dishonored.

In the first trailer for Guns of Eschaton, we get an in-depth look at this mystical, powerful Western shooter that uses a unique blend of first-person action and Souls-like difficulty and strategy to present something that truly stands out from the crowd.

Guns of Eschaton Can Be Wishlisted Now

The Guns of Eschaton trailer reveals stacks of information about this sumptuous shooter, which already has me very excited for what’s to come. It’s the final game from Viktor Antonov, a legendary developer who sadly passed away last year.

In Guns of Eschaton, players contend with a vast journey across a dying America in a reimagined rendition of the Old West. The apocalypse has blanketed the landscape, and players are forced to battle a range of enemies, spanning minor mobs to hulking, horrific creatures.

Here’s the reveal trailer for Guns of Eschaton:

In a statement, Fuad Kuliev, the head of the studio at Eschatology Entertainment, said:

We are incredibly honoured to be revealing Guns of Eschaton, the final project shaped by the extraordinary vision of Viktor Antonov.

From the earliest stages of development, I had the privilege of shaping this world together with Viktor: where ideas, themes, and concepts evolved through his talent into the world players will see today.

Viktor’s imagination and creative legacy have been a constant source of inspiration throughout development, and this game is the result of an incredible collaborative effort from our entire team to bring that vision to life. We are proud to share it with players around the world in partnership with our publisher, 4Divinity.

This game will truly challenge players, bringing the multi-dimensional difficulty elements of a Souls-like game into a traditional, first-person environment. It looks as though the pairing will work well, encouraging players to use various weapons, powers, and abilities as they customize and grow their character either alone or with allies.

Guns of Eschaton doesn’t have a release date, but it can be wishlisted now on PlayStation 5, XBOX, and PC.

Does Guns of Eschaton look like your kind of game? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that someone used Microsoft Rewards to get GTA 6 for free, and don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
